mission: Characterize exactly the admissible standard thermal-leptogenesis source region implied by the closed late-time IO eta target.

theorem_1_exact_admissible_region.statement: The closed late-time IO eta target fixes the exact admissible source region of standard hierarchical thermal leptogenesis. No further target ambiguity remains; every consistent source point must lie in this region.

theorem_2_exact_floors.statement: The exact absolute floors implied by the closed eta target are: |eps1| >= eps1_kappa_target, M1 >= M1_floor_absolute, and in a thermal scenario T_reh >= M1_floor_absolute.

theorem_3_poplawski_slice_characterizati.slice_assumption: identify the previously fixed external Poplawski freeze-out target with M1
theorem_3_poplawski_slice_characterizati.statement: On the slice M1 = T_f,target, the admissible region collapses to the interval kappa_f >= M1_floor_absolute / T_f,target, with equality only on Davidson-Ibarra saturation.

theorem_4_uniqueness_nogo.statement: Without additional source microphysics beyond the exact target product and the Davidson-Ibarra ceiling, the admissible source set remains a positive-dimensional region. Therefore no unique thermal-leptogenesis source point can be derived from the closed late-time IO eta target alone.
exact_remaining_external_debt: a source-sector theorem selecting a point inside the admissible region
or an exact microphysical law relating kappa_f and M1
or an exact source theorem fixing eps1 independently of the Davidson-Ibarra bound
